title: Investing Capital to Defend the Nation
body: Author: David Ulevitch
Focus: Capital investment in American industrial capacity as national security imperative

title: Agenda
body: U.S. technological leadership decline
New investment framework: Global Competition and Innovation (GCI)
Eight critical sectors requiring capital
Role of institutional investors

title: America's Technology Leadership Erosion
body: In the early 2000s, the U.S. led in 60 of 64 critical technologies
Now the U.S. leads in only seven critical technologies
This represents a dramatic decline in technological dominance over two decades

title: China's Technology Ascendancy
body: China now leads in 57 critical technologies
This marks a near-complete reversal from the early 2000s position
China's leadership spans the majority of critical technology domains

title: From ESG to GCI Framework
body: Ulevitch proposes reframing investment strategy from ESG (Environmental, Social, Governance)
New framework: Global Competition and Innovation (GCI)
GCI positions strategic industrial investment as core to national competitiveness

title: Critical Sector: Semiconductors
body: Semiconductors identified as one of eight critical sectors
Requires targeted capital investment for U.S. competitiveness
Foundational technology for national security and economic strength

title: Critical Sector: Energy
body: Energy identified as one of eight critical sectors
Energy scaling specifically highlighted as investment priority
Essential infrastructure for industrial capacity and national resilience

title: Critical Sector: Artificial Intelligence
body: AI identified as one of eight critical sectors
AI deployment specifically emphasized as investment target
Critical for maintaining technological and strategic advantage

title: Critical Sector: Biotechnology
body: Biotech identified as one of eight critical sectors
Part of the strategic industrial capacity framework
Relevant to both healthcare security and innovation leadership

title: Critical Sector: Space
body: Space identified as one of eight critical sectors
Represents frontier technology and strategic domain
Requires sustained capital commitment for U.S. leadership

title: Eight Critical Sectors Overview
body: Total of eight critical sectors identified for GCI investment focus
Five explicitly named: semiconductors, energy, AI, biotech, and space
These sectors form the core of American industrial capacity rebuilding

title: Institutional Investor Categories
body: Pension funds identified as key capital anchors
Sovereign wealth vehicles must participate in strategic investment
Insurance companies and endowments also designated as critical investors

title: Investment Priority: Manufacturing
body: Large institutional investors must anchor capital in manufacturing
Manufacturing capacity is foundational to industrial competitiveness
Direct link between manufacturing investment and national security

title: Investment Priority: AI Deployment
body: Large institutional investors must anchor capital in AI deployment
Deployment emphasis distinguishes this from pure research investment
Practical application of AI technology is strategic priority

title: Investment Priority: Energy Scaling
body: Large institutional investors must anchor capital in energy scaling
Scaling existing energy technologies is emphasized over pure innovation
Energy infrastructure expansion is critical for industrial capacity

title: Fiduciary Duty Reframed
body: Strategic industrial investment is financially rational fiduciary duty
This is not charity but sound investment strategy
National security alignment and financial returns are compatible
Institutional investors can fulfill fiduciary obligations while supporting national competitiveness
